summary: Handoffs for 'nano' scale tasks must explicitly define scope and limitations to remove ambiguity and prevent accidental over-implementation by weak or constrained models.
For 'giant' scale memory_os work, a comprehensive control-plane view must be established before adding further automation to prevent product and Memory OS tracks from diverging.
Portable Memory OS should expose a single, stable control CLI entry point, while retaining lower-level scripts for specialized operations.
Batch capsule processing to prevent token limit truncation and invalid JSON when handling large backlogs.
Append new draft nodes to nodes.jsonl incrementally during batch processing to enable reciprocal incremental nodes to be accessible in intermediate batch loops.
Expand lifecycle transition validation to support connector, config, and policy types beyond existing node types.

The Memory OS architecture must strictly separate Developer Memory OS from User-Facing Memory OS at the database level to prevent accidental leaks and ensure data integrity. User-facing feedback (ratings, comments) must be persisted in the isolated User-Facing Memory OS database as telemetry.
